name: Northeast Colorado Housing, Inc.
description_short: Non-profit housing organization in Northeast Colorado offering affordable housing solutions, down payment assistance, and HUD-approved home buyer counseling to rural families.
description_long:
Northeast Colorado Housing, Inc. is a non-profit organization based in Fort Morgan, Colorado, serving six rural counties in Northeast Colorado: Logan, Morgan, Phillips, Sedgwick, Washington, and Yuma. The organization focuses on addressing the critical need for safe and affordable housing in underserved rural communities where traditional lending and housing resources are limited.

The organization provides a comprehensive suite of housing-focused services including home rehabilitation loans, affordable rental properties through Fort Morgan Townhomes, down payment assistance loans for first-time homebuyers, and Medicaid home modifications. They also offer HUD-approved home buyer counseling programs, foreclosure counseling, and home buyer education classes both in-person and online. Additionally, they serve as a packaging provider for USDA Rural Development Direct Loans, helping rural borrowers access federal financing.

Northeast Colorado Housing distinguishes itself by combining direct financial assistance (loans and down payment help) with educational counseling services, addressing both immediate housing needs and long-term financial literacy. Their focus on rural development financing and Medicaid home modifications demonstrates specialization in underserved populations. The organization's self-help housing program and online education options extend accessibility beyond in-person appointments in a geographically sparse region.

As a free-help non-profit, the organization provides legitimate HUD-approved counseling without credit repair claims or predatory lending. The main limitation is geographic scope—services are restricted to six rural Northeast Colorado counties, making them inaccessible to consumers outside this region. Website content lacks information on counselor credentials, counseling costs (if any), or loan terms and rates.
pros: HUD-approved home buyer counseling program providing legitimate financial education
Offers down payment assistance loans, directly addressing primary barrier to homeownership
Serves six rural counties (Logan, Morgan, Phillips, Sedgwick, Washington, Yuma) with limited lending access
Provides both home rehabilitation loans and affordable rental properties—two pathways to housing stability
USDA Rural Development Direct Loan packaging helps borrowers access federal agricultural lending
Medicaid home modifications service addresses accessibility and safety needs for elderly/disabled residents
Online home buyer education classes increase accessibility in dispersed rural communities
cons: Geographic limitation: services only available in six Northeast Colorado counties; not accessible to majority of US consumers
Website lacks specific loan terms, interest rates, repayment periods, and eligibility requirements for down payment assistance and rehabilitation loans
No information provided on counselor credentials, certifications, or whether counseling services are free or fee-based
Limited contact information and no online application or inquiry system visible on website
No details on waitlists, processing timelines, or success rates for loan programs
best_for: First-time homebuyers in rural Northeast Colorado seeking down payment assistance and pre-purchase education
Homeowners needing rehabilitation financing or Medicaid accessibility modifications in the six-county service area
Rural borrowers preparing USDA Rural Development Direct Loan applications with local packaging support
diagnosis: Northeast Colorado Housing is best for rural families in six specific Northeast Colorado counties seeking affordable homeownership pathways combined with HUD-approved financial counseling. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—the organization does not serve consumers outside their rural service area, significantly limiting accessibility for most of the US population.
services: USDA Rural Development Direct Loan packaging
Housing services for Logan, Morgan, Phillips, Sedgwick, Washington, and Yuma counties
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 801 S West St Apt 25, , Fort Morgan, CO 80701
